title: DOM State, Events, and Accessible Interaction · Retrieval Circuit
summary: Build a keyboard-operable service-status interface whose DOM, focus, announcements, and visual state stay synchronized. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for an accessible interactive service-status interface after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Query, create, move, replace, and remove DOM nodes with explicit missing-element and ownership handling.
Derive DOM output from application state without duplicating conflicting sources of truth.
Trace event target, currentTarget, capture, bubble, composed paths, and cancellation before delegating behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.javascript:
// javascript-basics cumulative stakeholder program.
// Add one runnable behavior, assertion, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Dom Query Create evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Dom Query Create assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: An accessible status filter has 7 options, works by keyboard and pointer, survives dynamic list updates through delegation, restores focus after removal, and announces the changed result count.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Dom Query Create has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Dom Query Create precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: An accessible status filter has 7 options, works by keyboard and pointer, survives dynamic list updates through delegation, restores focus after removal, and announces the changed result count.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Changing an HTML string is always safer than constructing DOM nodes.

options.text: Declare inputs, observable output, ownership, and the runtime behavior being tested
Trace values, bindings, receivers, mutations, and queued work before editing code
Implement the smallest behavior and retain an assertion over the actual result
Run boundary and changed-event cases, inspect failures, and remove accidental coupling

stimulus.title: Live Updates case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: An accessible status filter has 4 options, works by keyboard and pointer, survives dynamic list updates through delegation, restores focus after removal, and announces the changed result count.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Moving focus to every updated message is the most accessible announcement.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Announce meaningful asynchronous changes without noisy live regions or focus theft. Artifact under review: an accessible interactive service-status interface.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Live Updates, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write the review evidence note for Live Updates: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — An accessible status filter has 5 options, works by keyboard and pointer, survives dynamic list updates through delegation, restores focus after removal, and announces the changed result count.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Announce meaningful asynchronous changes without noisy live regions or focus theft.
